name: Dialog
description: Modal dialog with header, content, footer, and focus trap.

api.name: Dialog
api.description: Modal dialog with header, content, footer, and focus trap.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The dialog's trigger and content, typically a DialogTrigger and DialogContent.

parameters.name: Open
parameters.description: Whether the dialog is open. Two-way bindable via OpenChanged; when neither this nor OpenChanged is supplied, the dialog manages its own state internally, seeded from DefaultOpen.

parameters.name: OpenChanged
parameters.description: Raised when the open state changes, e.g. via DialogTrigger, Escape, a backdrop click, or DialogClose.

parameters.name: DefaultOpen
parameters.description: Uncontrolled initial open state. Used only when the consumer does NOT bind Open (Radix defaultOpen): the dialog then owns its open state internally, seeded from this value. When Open is supplied the component is controlled and this is ignored.

parameters.name: OnBeforeClose
parameters.description: Fires before any user-initiated dismiss (Escape, backdrop click, X button, or a DialogClose trigger). Set Cancel to true to abort the dismiss — useful for "discard unsaved changes?" guards. Inspect Reason to find out which gesture asked to close.

parameters.name: IsOpen
parameters.description: Legacy alias for Open.

parameters.name: IsOpenChanged
parameters.description: Legacy alias for OpenChanged.

events.name: OpenChanged
events.description: Raised when the open state changes, e.g. via DialogTrigger, Escape, a backdrop click, or DialogClose.

events.name: OnBeforeClose
events.description: Fires before any user-initiated dismiss (Escape, backdrop click, X button, or a DialogClose trigger). Set Cancel to true to abort the dismiss — useful for "discard unsaved changes?" guards. Inspect Reason to find out which gesture asked to close.

records.name: DialogContext
records.signature: DialogContext(bool IsOpen, EventCallback<bool> SetOpen, string TitleId, string DescriptionId, Func<string, Task<bool>> TryDismiss, DialogDescriptionRegistry DescriptionRegistry = null!, Action RegisterDescription = null!, Action UnregisterDescription = null!)

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<Dialog @bind-Open="_isOpen">
    <DialogTrigger>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Edit Profile</Button>
    </DialogTrigger>
    <DialogContent>
        <DialogHeader>
            <DialogTitle>Edit Profile</DialogTitle>
            <DialogDescription>Make changes to your profile here.</DialogDescription>
        </DialogHeader>
        <Grid Columns="4" Gap="4" Class="items-center py-4">
            <Label Class="text-right">Name</Label>
            <Input Class="col-span-3" Value="John Doe" />
            <Label Class="text-right">Username</Label>
            <Input Class="col-span-3" Value="@johndoe" />
        </Grid>
        <DialogFooter>
            <DialogClose>
                <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Cancel</Button>
            </DialogClose>
            <DialogClose>
                <Button>Save Changes</Button>
            </DialogClose>
        </DialogFooter>
    </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

@code {
    private bool _isOpen;
}

examples.title: Simple Dialog
examples.code:
<Dialog @bind-Open="_isOpen">
    <DialogTrigger>
        <Button>Share</Button>
    </DialogTrigger>
    <DialogContent>
        <DialogHeader>
            <DialogTitle>Share this document</DialogTitle>
            <DialogDescription>Anyone with the link can view this document.</DialogDescription>
        </DialogHeader>
        <Stack Gap="4" Class="py-4">
            <Input Value="https://example.com/share/abc123" />
        </Stack>
        <DialogFooter>
            <DialogClose>
                <Button>Copy Link</Button>
            </DialogClose>
        </DialogFooter>
    </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

@code {
    private bool _isOpen;
}

examples.title: Confirmation Dialog
examples.code:
<Dialog @bind-Open="_isOpen">
    <DialogTrigger>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Destructive">
            <SvgGlyph Svg="@(Lucide.Trash)" class="h-4 w-4" /> Delete Account
        </Button>
    </DialogTrigger>
    <DialogContent>
        <DialogHeader>
            <DialogTitle>Are you absolutely sure?</DialogTitle>
            <DialogDescription>
                This action cannot be undone. This will permanently delete your
                account and remove your data from our servers.
            </DialogDescription>
        </DialogHeader>
        <DialogFooter>
            <DialogClose>
                <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Cancel</Button>
            </DialogClose>
            <DialogClose>
                <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Destructive">Yes, delete account</Button>
            </DialogClose>
        </DialogFooter>
    </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

@code {
    private bool _isOpen;
}

examples.title: Prevent Close
examples.code:
<Dialog @bind-Open="_preventCloseOpen">
    <DialogTrigger>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Open Protected Dialog</Button>
    </DialogTrigger>
    <DialogContent PreventClose="true">
        <DialogHeader>
            <DialogTitle>Unsaved Changes</DialogTitle>
            <DialogDescription>You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to leave?</DialogDescription>
        </DialogHeader>
        <DialogFooter>
            <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline" OnClick="@(() => _preventCloseOpen = false)">Discard</Button>
            <Button OnClick="@(() => _preventCloseOpen = false)">Save Changes</Button>
        </DialogFooter>
    </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

@code {
    private bool _preventCloseOpen;
}

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The close trigger's content, e.g. a "Cancel" button.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the trigger element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the trigger element.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The dialog panel's content, typically DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, and DialogFooter.

parameters.name: PreventClose
parameters.description: Disables the Escape key and backdrop click as dismiss gestures, and (unless ShowCloseButton forces it) hides the X button — leaving only an explicit DialogClose (or programmatic close) able to dismiss the dialog.

parameters.name: ShowCloseButton
parameters.description: Whether the top-end close (X) button renders. null (default) keeps the legacy coupling — X shows whenever PreventClose is false. true forces the X even on a modal dialog (backdrop/Escape stay disabled; the X still routes through the dismiss guard so an OnBeforeClose veto applies); false hides it for fully custom close chrome. Mirrors OverlayOptions.ShowCloseButton for service dialogs.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Controls the panel's max width, from Sm up to Full. Default Default.

parameters.name: Scrollable
parameters.description: Caps the panel at 85vh and makes its content vertically scrollable, so a tall body doesn't overflow the viewport.

parameters.name: PlayExitAnimation
parameters.description: When true (the default), closing the dialog keeps the panel mounted for a brief zoom-out (and backdrop fade-out) before unmounting, instead of vanishing instantly — so a declarative <DialogContent> animates its close the same way a declarative Sheet already does, and the same way OverlayProvider already animates service-opened dialogs. Set to false to opt OUT (immediate unmount on close) for tests or consumers who want an instant dismiss.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the dialog panel.

parameters.name: ZIndex
parameters.description: Backdrop z-index. Content sits at ZIndex + 1. Defaults to BaseZIndex; OverlayProvider supplies an incrementing value per nested overlay so a Dialog opened from inside another Dialog stacks above instead of colliding.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the dialog panel.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: The trigger's content, rendered inside the wrapper element (or folded onto the single child element when AsChild is set).

parameters.name: ChildContentSlot
parameters.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary markup — receives the TriggerSlot to splat manually. Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild.

parameters.name: AsChild
parameters.description: When true, renders NO wrapper and cascades a TriggerSlot a cooperating child (a Lumeo <Button>, or a manual ChildContentSlot) folds onto its own single root element — the Radix asChild goal: one DOM node, no nested-interactive markup. Prefer a <button> child (a <Button Href> link activates on Enter only, not Space). Default false keeps the current role=button wrapper.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS classes merged onto the trigger element (or the TriggerSlot passed to the AsChild child).

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the trigger element (or the TriggerSlot passed to the AsChild child).

mdSummary:
# Dialog

Modal dialog with header, content, footer, and focus trap.

**Package:** `Lumeo` · **Category:** Overlay
**Docs:** https://lumeo.nativ.sh/components/dialog

## Parameters
| Owner | Name | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Dialog | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | The dialog's trigger and content, typically a DialogTrigger and DialogContent. |
| Dialog | Open | `bool` | `false` | Whether the dialog is open. Two-way bindable via OpenChanged; when neither this nor OpenChanged is supplied, the dialog manages its own state internally, seeded from DefaultOpen. |
| Dialog | OpenChanged | `EventCallback<bool>` | `` | Raised when the open state changes, e.g. via DialogTrigger, Escape, a backdrop click, or DialogClose. |
| Dialog | DefaultOpen | `bool` | `false` | Uncontrolled initial open state. Used only when the consumer does NOT bind Open (Radix defaultOpen): the dialog then owns its open state internally, seeded from this value. When Open is supplied the component is controlled and this is ignored. |
| Dialog | OnBeforeClose | `EventCallback<DismissEventArgs>` | `` | Fires before any user-initiated dismiss (Escape, backdrop click, X button, or a DialogClose trigger). Set Cancel to true to abort the dismiss — useful for "discard unsaved changes?" guards. Inspect Reason to find out which gesture asked to close. |
| Dialog | IsOpen | `bool` | `false` | Legacy alias for Open. |
| Dialog | IsOpenChanged | `EventCallback<bool>` | `` | Legacy alias for OpenChanged. |
| DialogClose | Context | `Dialog.DialogContext` | `default!` |  |
| DialogClose | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | The close trigger's content, e.g. a "Cancel" button. |
| DialogClose | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the trigger element. |
| DialogClose | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the trigger element. |
| DialogContent | Context | `Dialog.DialogContext` | `default!` |  |
| DialogContent | Shell | `OverlayShellMarker?` | `` |  |
| DialogContent | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | The dialog panel's content, typically DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, and DialogFooter. |
| DialogContent | PreventClose | `bool` | `false` | Disables the Escape key and backdrop click as dismiss gestures, and (unless ShowCloseButton forces it) hides the X button — leaving only an explicit DialogClose (or programmatic close) able to dismiss the dialog. |
| DialogContent | ShowCloseButton | `bool?` | `` | Whether the top-end close (X) button renders. null (default) keeps the legacy coupling — X shows whenever PreventClose is false. true forces the X even on a modal dialog (backdrop/Escape stay disabled; the X still routes through the dismiss guard so an OnBeforeClose veto applies); false hides it for fully custom close chrome. Mirrors OverlayOptions.ShowCloseButton for service dialogs. |
| DialogContent | Size | `DialogSize` | `DialogSize.Default` | Controls the panel's max width, from Sm up to Full. Default Default. |
| DialogContent | Scrollable | `bool` | `false` | Caps the panel at 85vh and makes its content vertically scrollable, so a tall body doesn't overflow the viewport. |
| DialogContent | PlayExitAnimation | `bool` | `true` | When true (the default), closing the dialog keeps the panel mounted for a brief zoom-out (and backdrop fade-out) before unmounting, instead of vanishing instantly — so a declarative \<DialogContent\> animates its close the same way a declarative Sheet already does, and the same way OverlayProvider already animates service-opened dialogs. Set to false to opt OUT (immediate unmount on close) for tests or consumers who want an instant dismiss. |
| DialogContent | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the dialog panel. |
| DialogContent | ZIndex | `int` | `OverlayService.BaseZIndex` | Backdrop z-index. Content sits at ZIndex + 1. Defaults to BaseZIndex; OverlayProvider supplies an incrementing value per nested overlay so a Dialog opened from inside another Dialog stacks above instead of colliding. |
| DialogContent | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the dialog panel. |
| DialogDescription | Context | `Dialog.DialogContext` | `default!` |  |
| DialogDescription | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | The description text, rendered inside a \<p\> that DialogContent wires to aria-describedby for as long as this component is mounted. |
| DialogDescription | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the \<p\> element. |
| DialogDescription | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the \<p\> element. |
| DialogFooter | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | The footer's content, typically action buttons. |
| DialogFooter | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the footer \<div\>. |
| DialogFooter | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the footer \<div\>. |
| DialogHeader | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | The header's content, typically a DialogTitle and DialogDescription. |
| DialogHeader | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the header \<div\>. |
| DialogHeader | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the header \<div\>. |
| DialogTitle | Context | `Dialog.DialogContext` | `default!` |  |
| DialogTitle | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | The title text, rendered inside an \<h2\> that DialogContent wires to aria-labelledby. |
| DialogTitle | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the \<h2\> element. |
| DialogTitle | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the \<h2\> element. |
| DialogTrigger | Context | `Dialog.DialogContext` | `default!` |  |
| DialogTrigger | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | The trigger's content, rendered inside the wrapper element (or folded onto the single child element when AsChild is set). |
| DialogTrigger | ChildContentSlot | `RenderFragment<TriggerSlot>?` | `` | Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary markup — receives the TriggerSlot to splat manually. Lumeo \<Button\> children need only AsChild. |
| DialogTrigger | AsChild | `bool` | `false` | When true, renders NO wrapper and cascades a TriggerSlot a cooperating child (a Lumeo \<Button\>, or a manual ChildContentSlot) folds onto its own single root element — the Radix asChild goal: one DOM node, no nested-interactive markup. Prefer a \<button\> child (a \<Button Href\> link activates on Enter only, not Space). Default false keeps the current role=button wrapper. |
| DialogTrigger | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS classes merged onto the trigger element (or the TriggerSlot passed to the AsChild child). |
| DialogTrigger | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Captures any unmatched attributes and applies them to the trigger element (or the TriggerSlot passed to the AsChild child). |

## Slots
- `ChildContent` (Dialog) — The dialog's trigger and content, typically a DialogTrigger and DialogContent.
- `ChildContent` (DialogClose) — The close trigger's content, e.g. a "Cancel" button.
- `ChildContent` (DialogContent) — The dialog panel's content, typically DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, and DialogFooter.
- `ChildContent` (DialogDescription) — The description text, rendered inside a \<p\> that DialogContent wires to aria-describedby for as long as this component is mounted.
- `ChildContent` (DialogFooter) — The footer's content, typically action buttons.
- `ChildContent` (DialogHeader) — The header's content, typically a DialogTitle and DialogDescription.
- `ChildContent` (DialogTitle) — The title text, rendered inside an \<h2\> that DialogContent wires to aria-labelledby.
- `ChildContent` (DialogTrigger) — The trigger's content, rendered inside the wrapper element (or folded onto the single child element when AsChild is set).
- `ChildContentSlot` (DialogTrigger) — Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary markup — receives the TriggerSlot to splat manually. Lumeo \<Button\> children need only AsChild.

## Events
- `OpenChanged` (`EventCallback<bool>`) — Raised when the open state changes, e.g. via DialogTrigger, Escape, a backdrop click, or DialogClose.
- `OnBeforeClose` (`EventCallback<DismissEventArgs>`) — Fires before any user-initiated dismiss (Escape, backdrop click, X button, or a DialogClose trigger). Set Cancel to true to abort the dismiss — useful for "discard unsaved changes?" guards. Inspect Reason to find out which gesture asked to close.
- `IsOpenChanged` (`EventCallback<bool>`) — Legacy alias for OpenChanged.

## Sub-components
- `DialogClose`, `DialogContent`, `DialogDescription`, `DialogFooter`, `DialogHeader`, `DialogTitle`, `DialogTrigger`

## Cascading values it expects
- `Context` (`Dialog.DialogContext`)
- `Shell` (`OverlayShellMarker?`)

## Services it injects
- `IComponentInteropService` (Lumeo.Services)
- `IComponentInteropService` ()
- `ILumeoLocalizer` (Lumeo.Services.Localization)

## Keyboard
- `Escape` — HandleKeyDown handles Escape in DialogContent.razor

## Example
```razor
<Dialog />
```

## CSS variables
`--color-background`, `--color-border`, `--color-muted`, `--color-muted-foreground`, `--color-ring`, `--radius`

## Files
- `UI/Dialog/Dialog.razor`
- `UI/Dialog/DialogClose.razor`
- `UI/Dialog/DialogContent.razor`
- `UI/Dialog/DialogDescription.razor`
- `UI/Dialog/DialogFooter.razor`
- `UI/Dialog/DialogHeader.razor`
- `UI/Dialog/DialogTitle.razor`
- `UI/Dialog/DialogTrigger.razor`
